sanskrit: मत्कर्मकृन्मत्परमो मद्भक्तः सङ्गवर्जितः । निर्वैरः सर्वभूतेषु यः स मामेति पाण्डव ॥
hindiTranslation: हे पाण्डवन  जो मेरे लिये ही कर्म करनेवाला, मेरे ही परायण और मेरा ही भक्त है तथा सर्वथा आसक्तिरहित और प्राणिमात्रके साथ निर्वैर है, वह भक्त मेरेको प्राप्त होता है ॥
englishTranslation: O Pandava, the devotee who works for Me alone, takes Me as the highest goal, is devoted to Me, is free from attachment, and bears no enmity toward any being, comes to Me.
essence: Devotion matures into freedom when attachment and hostility are gone.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The one who serves Krishna with full devotion, without attachment or hostility toward any being, reaches him.
simpleInsight: Devotion becomes complete when love is steady, desire is loose, and the heart stops making enemies.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna has already seen Krishna's cosmic form and heard that devotion is the only way to know him fully. Now Krishna gives the final shape of that devotion: act for him, hold him as the highest, free yourself from attachment, and drop hostility toward all beings.
modernRelevance: You keep replaying the argument in your head after the meeting ends. The verse points to a cleaner way to live: act from devotion, stop gripping the result, and let resentment fall away.
translationLiteral: मत्कर्मकृत् (doing action for Me) / मत्परमः (having Me as the highest) / मद्भक्तः (My devotee) / सङ्गवर्जितः (free from attachment) / निर्वैरः (without hostility) / सर्वभूतेषु (toward all beings) / यः (who) / सः (that one) / माम् (Me) / एति (comes) / पाण्डव (O Pandava)
insight: It shifts devotion from feeling alone to a whole way of living: action, priority, detachment, and goodwill.

quoteVariants.text: Work for the divine, stay free of attachment, and carry no enmity. Such a heart reaches the divine.
No attachment, no hostility — that is the way home.
